li2022.author: Li, C.X., Liu, Y., Zhang, Y.Z., Li, J.C. and Lai, J.
li2022.title: Astragalus polysaccharide: a review of its immunomodulatory effect

zhang2019.author: Zhang, J., Wu, C., Gao, L., Du, G. and Qin, X.
zhang2019.title: Astragaloside IV derived from Astragalus membranaceus: A research review on the pharmacological effects

actions.action: Immune support / immunomodulation (activates macrophages, NK cells, T and B lymphocytes)
Adaptogenic tonic for fatigue and low vitality (traditional 'Qi' tonic)
Supports resistance to colds and respiratory infection (immune)
Renoprotective (kidney support)
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory

constituents.name: Cycloartane triterpenoid saponins (astragaloside IV)
constituents.note: Major bioactive saponin with cardio-, neuro-, hepato- and renoprotective effects.

constituents.name: Isoflavonoids (calycosin, formononetin)
constituents.note: Antioxidant flavonoid constituents of the root.

contraindications.note: Astragalus stimulates immune activity; on theoretical grounds it should be used cautiously in autoimmune disease and with immunosuppressant medicines (for example after organ transplant) - discuss with a clinician.
Generally well tolerated; high-quality long-term human safety and toxicity data remain limited.
Safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding has not been established; avoid medicinal doses.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Archives of Pharmacal Research review of Astragalus polysaccharide; Advances in Pharmacology review of astragaloside IV). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (immune-support, fatigue, cold, kidney-support); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

steenkamp2005.author: Steenkamp, V., Gouws, M.C., Gulumian, M., Elgorashi, E.E. and van Staden, J.
steenkamp2005.title: Studies on antibacterial, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activity of herbal remedies used in the treatment of benign prostatic hyperplasia and prostatitis

coulson2013.author: Coulson, S., Rao, A., Beck, S.L., Steels, E., Gramotnev, H. and Vitetta, L.
coulson2013.title: A phase II randomised double-blind placebo-controlled clinical trial investigating the efficacy and safety of ProstateEZE Max: a herbal medicine preparation (containing Epilobium parviflorum) for the management of symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy

actions.action: Supports lower urinary tract symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (prostate support)
Anti-inflammatory (inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 prostaglandin synthesis)
Antibacterial, including against Escherichia coli (urinary support)
Antioxidant (hydroxyl-radical scavenging)

constituents.name: Macrocyclic ellagitannins (notably oenothein B)
constituents.note: Characteristic Epilobium polyphenols underlying the anti-inflammatory and prostate activity.

constituents.name: Plant sterols (beta-sitosterol)
constituents.note: Sterols associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia support.

contraindications.note: Lower urinary tract / prostate symptoms must be medically assessed first to exclude prostate cancer; willowherb is a supportive measure, not a substitute for diagnosis and treatment.
Well tolerated over three months in clinical use; longer-term safety data are limited.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Journal of Ethnopharmacology activity study; Complementary Therapies in Medicine RCT). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (prostate-support, uti, inflammation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Arctostaphylos uva-ursi (L.) Spreng., folium (Uvae ursi folium)

hudek2022.author: Hudek Turkovic, A., Gunjaca, M., Marjanovic, M., Lovric, M., Butorac, A., Rasic, D., Peraica, M., Vujcic Bok, V., Sola, I., Rusak, G. and Durgo, K.
hudek2022.title: Proteome changes in human bladder T24 cells induced by hydroquinone derived from Arctostaphylos uva-ursi herbal preparation

actions.action: Urinary antiseptic for mild, uncomplicated lower urinary tract infection / cystitis (short-term)
Antibacterial in the urinary tract (arbutin is converted to antibacterial hydroquinone, favoured by alkaline urine)
Astringent (tannins)

constituents.name: Arbutin (and methylarbutin)
constituents.note: Hydroquinone glycoside; hydrolysed and excreted as antibacterial hydroquinone in the urine.

constituents.name: Tannins (gallotannins)
constituents.note: Up to ~20%; responsible for the astringency and for gastric irritation at high doses.

contraindications.note: Short-term use only: not for more than 1 week at a time and not more than about 5 times per year. If symptoms persist beyond ~4 days, worsen, or are accompanied by fever, blood in the urine or pain, seek medical care.
Contraindicated in pregnancy and breastfeeding and not for use in children or adolescents under 18 years.

Works best when the urine is alkaline; avoid taking with substances that acidify the urine. The hydroquinone released can be irritant/potentially hepatotoxic, so prolonged or repeated use must be avoided.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific and regulatory literature (EMA HMPC monograph, ESCOP monograph, Journal of Ethnopharmacology toxicology study). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (uti, infection); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited. NOTE strict short-term-use and pregnancy contraindications.

canter2004.author: Canter, P.H. and Ernst, E.
canter2004.title: Anthocyanosides of Vaccinium myrtillus (bilberry) for night vision - a systematic review of placebo-controlled trials

chan2020.author: Chan, S.W. and Tomlinson, B.
chan2020.title: Effects of Bilberry Supplementation on Metabolic and Cardiovascular Disease Risk

actions.action: Traditional support for eye strain and vision (note: rigorous trials do NOT support improved night vision in healthy eyes)
Supports capillary integrity and venous tone (traditional use for varicose veins / chronic venous insufficiency)
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory
Supports cardiovascular and metabolic risk factors, including blood lipids (high cholesterol)

constituents.name: Anthocyanins (anthocyanosides)
constituents.note: The deep blue-purple pigments; the principal antioxidant constituents.

constituents.name: Other flavonoids and tannins
constituents.note: Contribute to antioxidant and astringent activity.

constituents.name: Vitamin C and organic acids
constituents.note: Minor nutritive constituents of the berry.

contraindications.note: The berry is a food and generally very safe; long-term safety of concentrated high-dose extracts is less well characterised.
May lower blood sugar and has mild antiplatelet potential, so use cautiously alongside antidiabetic or anticoagulant/antiplatelet medication.
Claims that bilberry improves night vision in people with normal sight are not supported by rigorous clinical trials.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Survey of Ophthalmology systematic review; Molecules review of metabolic/cardiovascular effects). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (eye-strain, varicose-veins, high-cholesterol, inflammation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

review2020.title: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activity of bistort (Bistorta officinalis) aqueous extract and its major components - justification of the usage of the medicinal plant material as a traditional topical agent

webmd.author: WebMD
webmd.title: Bistort: Overview, Uses, Side Effects, Precautions, Interactions, Dosing and Reviews

actions.action: Astringent for mild diarrhoea (tannins tighten the gut lining and reduce excess secretion)
Anti-inflammatory gargle / mouthwash for inflammation of the mouth and throat (sore throat)
Vulnerary and styptic for minor wounds (topical)
Antibacterial (topical)

constituents.name: Tannins (15-36% of the rhizome: oligomeric proanthocyanidins, gallotannins, catechins)
constituents.note: Very high tannin content is the basis of the strong astringency.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids and flavonoids
constituents.note: Contribute to antioxidant and antibacterial activity.

contraindications.note: Very tannin-rich: high or prolonged intake may cause stomach upset or constipation and can reduce the absorption of iron and some medicines taken at the same time.
Traditional use only; diarrhoea persisting beyond a few days, or mouth/throat inflammation that does not settle, needs medical assessment.
Safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not established; avoid medicinal doses.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific and general literature (Journal of Ethnopharmacology activity study; WebMD monograph). Bistorta officinalis = Persicaria bistorta = Polygonum bistorta. Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (diarrhea, sore-throat, wounds); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.
